act_name: Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act 2013
text: The Land Acquisition Act 2013 applies when the government acquires private land for public purposes. Government must give you FOUR TIMES the market value for rural land and TWO TIMES for urban land as compensation. Steps when government acquires your land: (1) Preliminary notification under Section 11; (2) Social Impact Assessment study (3) Declaration under Section 19; (4) Compensation award. You have the right to object during the SIA stage. Farmers' consent required for PPP projects and private companies acquiring farmland.

No person shall enter into any benami transaction. A benami property means any property held by or transferred to a person but paid for by another person. Benami property can be confiscated by the government. If you suspect someone holding property in your name against your will or without consent, report to the Adjudicating Authority. Punishment for benami transactions: rigorous imprisonment 1 to 7 years and fine up to 25% of fair market value.

section: Section 31 — Filing of Complaints with RERA Authority
chapter: Chapter V — Real Estate Regulatory Authority
text: If a builder/developer delays possession of your flat, changes building specifications, takes extra money, or fails to give promised amenities, you can file a complaint with RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Authority) in your state. File at your state RERA website. The builder must register the project with RERA before selling. RERA can order the builder to: refund your money with interest, pay compensation for delay, complete the project. Penalty on builder can be up to 10% of project cost. If builder still doesn't comply, imprisonment up to 3 years.

section: Section 18 — Return of amount and compensation
chapter: Chapter III — Functions and Duties of Promoter
text: If the promoter fails to complete or is unable to give possession of an apartment, plot or building on the date specified in the agreement, the allottee (buyer) shall be entitled to: (1) Withdraw from the project and get full refund with interest at SBI MCLR + 2% from date of each payment; OR (2) Continue in the project and get monthly interest for delay until possession. You cannot be forced to accept a lesser refund. Approach RERA within 3 years of the cause of complaint. RERA orders must be complied with within 60 days.

act_name: Indian Stamp Act 1899 and Registration Act 1908
text: Sale deed, gift deed, lease deed must be registered at the Sub-Registrar's office for transfer of property to be legally valid. Stamp duty is a state subject and varies by state — typically 4-7% of the property value. Registration charges are 1% of property value. Without registration, the transfer is NOT legally valid and you cannot claim ownership in court. Documents required for registration: original sale deed, id proof of buyer and seller, PAN cards, property tax receipts, NOC from society/builder if apartment. After registration, get the property mutated in your name in the municipal records.
